description: Atoms are the building blocks of skills. Each atom represents a single effect that the engine interprets. A skill can have multiple atoms executed in sequence.

move.description: Applies movement to the caster. Sub-kinds: directional (walk), jump, dash.

camera.description: Rotates the camera. Axis: yaw or pitch.

spawn_object.description: Instantiates a reusable scene asset at a position relative to the caster. The object is a trigger (Area3D) — it detects overlaps but has no physics response.

params.asset: Path to the .tscn asset to instantiate.
params.offset: Position relative to the caster's facing direction (forward, right, up).
params.dimensions: Scale of the spawned object relative to the asset's base size.
params.duration: How long the object lives before being freed (seconds). 0 = instant.

push.description: Applies a velocity impulse to all bodies overlapping a trigger. Direction is the caster's forward. Force is calculated from skill speed and caster weight.

params.force: Base push force (speed of the skill).
params.weight_multiplier: If true, force is multiplied by the caster's weight attribute.

hit.description: Composite atom: spawn_object + push. Spawns a trigger hitbox and pushes anything inside it. This is a convenience atom that combines spawn_object and push into one.

trigger.description: A trigger is an Area3D node that detects overlaps with physics bodies but does not push them. Used for hit detection, zones, and area effects.
trigger.implementation: Godot: Area3D with collision_layer=0, collision_mask set to target layers. On body_entered → apply effect.
trigger.reusable_assets: Trigger objects (hitbox_cube.tscn, hitbox_sphere.tscn) are stored in res://assets/ and reused across skills. Only dimensions and spawn offset change per skill.
